sourceRef.citation: Kourovka Notebook, Problem 5.10 (Khukhro & Mazurov eds., ed.5)

statement.latex: Let$E=H*K$and denote the augmentation ideals of the groups$E$,$H$,$K$by${ e}$,${ h}$,${ k}$, respectively. If$I$is a right ideal in${ Z}E$, let$d_E(I)$denote the minimum number of generators of$I$as a right ideal. Assuming$H$and$K$finitely generated, is it true that$d_E({ e})=d_E({ h}E) + d_E({ k}E)?$Here${ h}E$is, of course, the right ideal generated by${ h}$; similarly for${ k}E$. { K.\,W.\,Grue...

reiAssessment.solveRationale: Group theory VI_BRIDGING; Rei not specialized in group theory (yet)
reiAssessment.formalizationNotes: Kourovka problems are typically research-level group theory
